Section Title: Energy Thematic Funds > How Thematic Energy Mutual Funds Work?
Content:
Energy mutual funds invest in stocks of companies operating in the energy sector, including oil, [power generation & distribution](https://www.etmoney.com/stocks/sector/power-generation-distribution/286 "power sector") , natural gas, renewable energy, and utilities. These funds aim to provide growth by capitalizing on energy price movements and industry trends. Their performance is influenced by factors like global energy demand, geopolitical events, and regulatory policies. Investors choose energy mutual funds for sector-specific diversification and potential long-term returns, though they come with volatility risks.
 ...

results.title: How to Invest in Uranium in India – Best Ways, Risks & Benefits
excerpts:
Section Title: **Can You Directly Invest in Uranium in India?**
Content:
In India, **retail investors cannot directly buy or trade physical uranium** due to regulatory and safety restrictions. Uranium mining and processing are highly regulated and typically controlled by governments.
**However, investors can gain exposure through:**
Uranium mining company stocks
Global uranium ETFs
Nuclear energy companies
Mutual funds with international exposure
Thematic clean energy funds
 ... 
Section Title: **Can You Directly Invest in Uranium in India?** > **2️** **.** **Invest in Uranium ETFs**
Content:
Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) offer diversified exposure to uranium mining and nuclear energy companies.
Examples :
**Global X Uranium ETF**
**Sprott Uranium Miners ETF**
These ETFs track uranium-related companies rather than physical uranium itself.
**3️** **.** **Invest in Nuclear Energy Companies**
Instead of pure uranium miners, you can invest in companies involved in:
**Nuclear power plant construction**
**Uranium processing**
**Nuclear fuel services**
**4️. Invest via International Mutual Funds**
Some Indian mutual funds provide exposure to global clean energy or commodity themes, indirectly including uranium-linked stocks.
**5️** **.** **Invest in Indian Nuclear & Energy Companies**
While India does not have publicly listed uranium mining companies, you may consider companies involved in:
**Power generation**
**Infrastructure**
**Engineering linked to nuclear power projects**
 ...

Section Title: ... > Union Budget 2026-27 Announcements Affecting the Rare Earth & Mining Sector in India
Content:
Nirmala Sitharaman used Budget 2026–27 to recognise rare earth elements and critical minerals as strategic inputs for technology, defence, EVs and advanced manufacturing. The Budget introduced initiatives focused on self-sufficiency in mineral extraction, processing and value addition, with a strong push through the creation of Dedicated Rare Earth Corridors.
Below are the highlights for rare earth & mining.
Section Title: ... > Union Budget 2026-27 Announcements Affecting the Rare Earth & Mining Sector in India
Content:
**Dedicated Rare Earth Corridors announced:** The Budget supports mineral-rich states such as Odisha, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu to develop integrated corridors covering mining, processing, research and manufacturing.
**Value chain development for critical minerals:** The Budget focuses on building domestic industrial chains for rare earths that support EVs, electronics, defence and renewable energy sectors.
**Incentives for permanent magnet production:** The Budget provides support for rare earth permanent magnet manufacturing, which is essential for engines and advanced mobility systems.
**Mining policy alignment with global competitiveness:** The Budget shifts mining policy to reduce import dependence and strengthen India’s position in global critical mineral supply chains.
 ...

results.title: The Rise of Silver, Lithium, and Energy-Thematic Mutual Funds
excerpts:
Section Title: The Rise of Silver, Lithium, and Energy-Thematic Mutual Funds
Content:
Marisha Bhatt · 07 Oct 2025 · 9 mins read · 16 Comments
Share this blog
As the world shifts toward advanced technologies and clean energy, metals like silver, lithium, and rare earth minerals are becoming the new gold. This surge in demand has sparked a wave of theme-based mutual funds focused on these critical resources. Check out this blog to know why these energy and metal-focused funds are gaining traction and how they could add a spark to your investment portfolio.

results.title: Mining & Mineral products Stocks: List of Mining & Mineral products Shares in India | Angel One
excerpts:
Home ›
[Stocks](https://www.angelone.in/stocks) ›
[Mining & Mineral products Stocks](https://www.angelone.in/stocks/sector/mining-and-mineral-products)
 ... 
Section Title: Mining & Mineral products Stocks > About the Mining and Mineral Products Sector
Content:
Minerals are precious natural resources that are important raw materials for several industries, such as construction and manufacturing. Therefore, the growth of the mining industry is important for the country's overall industrial development. India is mainly self-sufficient in metallic minerals, such as chromites, bauxite, iron ore, lignite, etc. The industry has the potential to impact India’s GDP growth and foreign exchange earnings immensely.
In FY 2023, the overall coal production in India saw a surge to 893.08 MT from 728.72 MT in FY 2019. India is the third largest energy-consuming country globally, where the demand for electricity is high, hence the demand for coal. India produces 95 minerals, including 4 fuel, 23 non-metallic, 10 metallic, 3 atomic and 55 minor minerals.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Things To Consider Before Investing in the Mining and Mineral Sector
Content:
**Resource assessment** : Evaluate the country's mineral resources, considering the diversity of metallic and non-metallic minerals. Assess the potential for growth and sustainability in mining operations. **Regulatory environment:** It is very important to understand the regulatory framework governing mining activities. Stay informed about changes in policies, licenses, and environmental regulations affecting the sector. **Global demand:** Monitor global trends in mineral demand, especially for key minerals like coal, iron ore, and rare earth metals. Assess how international dynamics may influence the sector. High demand can impact the company’s profitability. **Infrastructure development:** Consider the impact of infrastructure development on mining operations. Adequate transportation and logistics are crucial for efficient mineral extraction and distribution.

Section Title: Nuclear Power's Gold Rush: 5 Stocks to Watch in 2026 > ... > \ Larsen & Toubro (L&T)
Content:
[L&T](https://www.equitymaster.com/share-price/LART/LT-500510/LT-Share-Price) is a global EPC leader and [India's engineering giant](https://www.equitymaster.com/detail.asp?date=01/01/2025&story=5&title=Where-Will-LT-Share-Price-be-in-3-Years) . Its heavy engineering department provides reactor pressure vessels, steam generators, and specialized forgings for India's nuclear plants.
It has implemented critical projects for the Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited and works with global reactor suppliers, too. Nuclear energy is part of L&T's Rs 6.13 trillion order book as of June 2025.
In FY25, the revenue grew 16% YoY to Rs 2,557 bn, while profit after tax grew 13.6% YoY to Rs 176.8 bn. The company's order book crossed Rs 6,128 bn, with a small contribution from nuclear infrastructure.
